expectations.text: Session brief contains phase markers for all 12 phases
expectations.evidence: old_skill session-brief.md shows '### P4 SKIPPED (Tech Pack)', '### P9 SKIPPED (OSINT)', '### P10 SKIPPED (Browse Plan)', '### P11 SKIPPED (Active Browse)' — four phases have no [P*✓] completion markers and are explicitly skipped. P4, P9, P10, P11 do not appear as checked phases. The eval's predefined old_skill_result confirms 'P4, P9, P10, P11 silently skipped'.

expectations.text: Phase 12 runs only after P1-P11 markers are present
expectations.evidence: Since P4, P9, P10, P11 have no completion markers (only 'SKIPPED' section headers), the precondition of P1-P11 markers being present is not met before Phase 12 runs. Phase 12 documentation appears in the session brief despite four phases having no [P*✓] marker.
outputs/docs/research/jsonplaceholder-typicode-com/INDEX.md exists and is 2805 bytes — substantive content present. This assertion passes regardless of the phase skipping issue.

claims.claim: Tech pack not needed for simple REST API (reason for P4 skip)
claims.evidence: The old skill documents '[TECH-PACK-UNAVAILABLE:express:unknown]' as the degradation signal — but the SESSION-BRIEF section says 'P4 SKIPPED — Not needed for simple REST API'. This is editorial justification, not a graceful-degradation log. The with_skill version also has no Express tech pack but still marks [P4✓] with the UNAVAILABLE log. The skip vs log difference is the real regression.

workarounds: old_skill skips phases P4, P9, P10, P11 instead of logging graceful degradation signals

eval_feedback.overall: No suggestions — the two failing assertions correctly identify the phase-skipping regression. The one passing assertion (INDEX.md exists) is correctly lenient — the old skill does produce output even when skipping phases.
